description: Fix scripts/dev.ts to read the WebSocket authentication secret from the correct file (auth.json instead of config.json), so that extension reload signals in dev mode authenticate successfully.

userStories.title: Fix readWsSecret() in scripts/dev.ts to read from auth.json
userStories.description: As a platform contributor using npm run dev, I want the extension reload signal to authenticate correctly so that the Chrome extension reloads automatically after tsc recompiles.
acceptanceCriteria: reloadExtension() sends the correct Bearer token to POST /extension/reload
If auth.json does not exist yet (first run before server starts), readWsSecret() returns null gracefully
userStories.notes: Bug location: scripts/dev.ts lines 129-142. The readWsSecret() function reads from join(homedir(), '.opentabs', 'config.json') and looks for a 'secret' field. But the auth secret is stored in ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json as { "secret": "<hex>" } — config.json does not contain a secret field. This means reloadExtension() (line 217) always sends an unauthenticated request (no Authorization header), and POST /extension/reload returns 401 (see platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts line 377 which calls checkBearerAuth). The fix: change line 131 from join(homedir(), '.opentabs', 'config.json') to join(homedir(), '.opentabs', 'extension', 'auth.json'). The JSON structure is the same — both would have a 'secret' key — but only auth.json actually contains it. Reference: every other file in the codebase reads the secret from auth.json (see platform/cli/src/config.ts line 75, platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts line 230, platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts line 380).
